reasoning: The baseline probability for a stock's unadjusted price to strictly increase over a 1-week to 1-year horizon ranges from ~51% to ~57%. Public Storage (PSA) has a strong dividend yield (~4%), meaning its capital distribution creates a mechanical drag on price appreciation, constraining long-term price returns relative to total returns. Recent price action places PSA around $286, which is toward the lower end of its 3-month trading range ($280-$298) and down from its $330s peak in late 2024. Ex-dividend distributions of ~$3 per share per quarter (which just happened on March 13, settling before our March 16 baseline) will occur around mid-June, mid-September, and mid-December, effectively suppressing step-payout close prices on these boundary dates. Given the absence of a strong short-term catalyst and acknowledging the unpredictable nature of near-term prices, estimates adhere firmly to market base-rates, starting at 51% for the short term and gently increasing to 56% at 1 year out to account for standard compound drift.
